title: Living in Boulder vlog 11 -  11 - 2027: Almost done linking Battles for Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a morning vlog update on his progress linking battles to his historical timeline project, currently at around 120 of 185 entries from the 2000s. He also discusses upcoming plans to catalog 2,700 cathedrals using Flickr, Wikipedia, and Google Earth, and mentions a long-running Google Earth Rome project he started in December 2016.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Uses a 'two or three week rule' — work on something for a few weeks, assess progress, keep going if happy
Perfectionist about his videos; wants to show all buildings before releasing a Rome tour video
Plans to shelve it temporarily while working on cathedrals and Rome, then return to it

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log. It's 6.23 in the morning on November 12th, 2017. So I guess I'll label this one as November 11th.
I got a haircut the other day. My mom took me out to get a haircut and then she got me like $30 worth of food. Some fancy food like beet chips.
And then I haven't done a video for the last couple of days. I can't remember where I was. I submitted a video somewhere in the 1800s or 1900s.
But yeah, I'm almost done with the battles now. I'm at like 120 or something of 185 from the 2000s.
And I'm putting that in the future because I actually don't have the ones that I have because I was working on it until like in 2011.
And so 2011 is actually the future from when I had it there. So I have to create new pages.
So I just put them in the future and I'll deal with that later.

Um, what else was I doing? Yeah, just those. Um, so yeah, I'll finish that tomorrow. Get up tomorrow.
I want to get to sleep before the sun comes up so I can feel like I got to sleep at night.
Um, but, uh, tomorrow I'll finish that pretty, and then I'll decide what I can, what I'm going to go from there.
I think I am going to do that. Go ahead and do that. At least get started with the castles and confusions.
I have to, I have to, um, start from scratch. I have my, I'm going to know which ones to do from the, uh, the photos of them on Wikipedia.
I mean, on, uh, Flickr. And then from that, I'll go to Wikipedia and then find out where they are and then do the place of them for Google Earth.
And then attach the photo to those. And then attach that to my website because I have them already on my website.
And then I'll, um, once I do all that with all that castles, there's 2700 castles and all that cathedrals.
Once I'm done with all of that, I'm going to ditch my timeline and I'll come back to it later.
Um, so hopefully I can do all that. It's going to be interesting how quick I can do all that. Hopefully I can do it. Oh, no, no, no.
I think when I'm done with that, I might, if I feel like I get done with that quickly, I might go ahead and do the archaeological sites and other stuff.
Cause I have other historical photos and I might attach all that. But yeah, and then I'll ditch it.
But yeah, if I can, so maybe I, so yeah, I got like another two weeks. The interesting thing is, um, I started working on Google Earth thing, um, Rome halfway through December.
I just remember that just off the top of my head, um, last year in 2016. And so I worked on that for a couple of months.
I don't even remember what I did after that. I was doing like, um, you know, uh, all the whole ancient books until the fall of Rome or on my website.

But the problem is I'm like a perfectionist. And so if I do a video, I want it to be good, you know?

I wanted to do a video of it where I can show all the other buildings and stuff.
But, yeah, so maybe it's possible to finish Rome. But I might get carried away and try and do stuff that's really complicated, like timing to me with it.
